description:
You are helping a script developer test some upcoming compression utilities.  
Inside the workstation you will find the working directory

/home/user/projects/archive-utils

It already contains three sub-directories that the utilities will later process:

/home/user/projects/archive-utils/alpha  
/home/user/projects/archive-utils/beta  
/home/user/projects/archive-utils/gamma  

Each of those sub-directories currently holds exactly two small text files.  
Your job is to exercise standard Linux compression and extraction commands so the developer can compare the behaviour of their utilities with what you do by hand.

Required end-state:

1. Create a new directory called  
   /home/user/projects/archive-utils/archives  
   It must hold three compressed archives:
   • alpha.tar.gz containing the entire contents of the alpha directory.  
   • beta.tar.gz  containing the entire contents of the beta  directory.  
   • gamma.tar.gz containing the entire contents of the gamma directory.  

2. After confirming that beta.tar.gz was produced, completely delete the original directory  
   /home/user/projects/archive-utils/beta  
   (The directory and all of its contents must be gone.)

3. Immediately restore that same directory by extracting beta.tar.gz so that, when you are finished, the directory tree looks exactly as it did at the beginning (alpha, beta and gamma are present and contain their original two files each).

4. Create a plain-text log file named  
   /home/user/projects/archive-utils/archive_activity.log  

   Append one log line after every significant step you perform.  
   The developer’s automated tests will read this file, so follow this exact format:

   step_number:ACTION:TARGET

   • step_number is a monotonically increasing positive integer starting at 1.  
   • ACTION is one of the following literal tokens (all capitals, no spaces):  
     COMPRESS, DELETE_DIR, EXTRACT, VERIFY_ARCHIVES, VERIFY_DIRS  
   • TARGET is:
     – for COMPRESS: the directory name you just compressed (alpha, beta or gamma)  
     – for DELETE_DIR: the directory name you deleted (beta)  
     – for EXTRACT: the archive file you extracted (beta.tar.gz)  
     – for VERIFY_ARCHIVES: a comma-separated list in the exact order alpha.tar.gz,beta.tar.gz,gamma.tar.gz  
     – for VERIFY_DIRS: a comma-separated list in the exact order alpha,beta,gamma  

   Therefore, if you work strictly in the order outlined above, the final
   /home/user/projects/archive-utils/archive_activity.log file must contain exactly these seven lines and nothing else:

   1:COMPRESS:alpha  
   2:COMPRESS:beta  
   3:COMPRESS:gamma  
   4:DELETE_DIR:beta  
   5:EXTRACT:beta.tar.gz  
   6:VERIFY_ARCHIVES:alpha.tar.gz,beta.tar.gz,gamma.tar.gz  
   7:VERIFY_DIRS:alpha,beta,gamma  

5. Make sure the permissions of every newly created file or directory allow the current non-root user to read and write them (the default umask settings are fine).

Hints for yourself (do NOT write these into the log file):
• You can use any reasonable combination of tar, gzip, rm, mkdir, and ls/echo for checks.  
• VERIFICATION steps (6 and 7) mean you, as the agent, should programmatically confirm the expected files/directories exist and then immediately append the log line.

The automated grader will check:

• That archives/ exists and contains exactly the three .tar.gz files.  
• That all three top-level directories (alpha, beta, gamma) exist and each still has its two original files.  
• That /home/user/projects/archive-utils/archive_activity.log exists and matches the seven-line specification above byte-for-byte (including newline characters).

Finish only when all criteria are met.
truth:
Initial filesystem provided to the agent before it starts:

Directories:
/home/user/projects/archive-utils/
/home/user/projects/archive-utils/alpha/
/home/user/projects/archive-utils/beta/
/home/user/projects/archive-utils/gamma/

Files and their contents (single trailing newline each):

/home/user/projects/archive-utils/alpha/file1.txt
Alpha file 1\n

/home/user/projects/archive-utils/alpha/file2.txt
Alpha file 2\n

/home/user/projects/archive-utils/beta/file1.txt
Beta file 1\n

/home/user/projects/archive-utils/beta/file2.txt
Beta file 2\n

/home/user/projects/archive-utils/gamma/file1.txt
Gamma file 1\n

/home/user/projects/archive-utils/gamma/file2.txt
Gamma file 2\n

Nothing else exists inside /home/user/projects/archive-utils at start.

Expected final state after agent finishes:

1. Directory /home/user/projects/archive-utils/archives/ exists.

2. Inside it, three archives exist:
   /home/user/projects/archive-utils/archives/alpha.tar.gz
   /home/user/projects/archive-utils/archives/beta.tar.gz
   /home/user/projects/archive-utils/archives/gamma.tar.gz

   Each archive, when extracted, reproduces exactly the corresponding original directory with its two text files and their original contents.

3. Directories:
/home/user/projects/archive-utils/alpha/
/home/user/projects/archive-utils/beta/
/home/user/projects/archive-utils/gamma/
   all exist and each still owns its two original files with identical contents.

4. Log file:
/home/user/projects/archive-utils/archive_activity.log
Exact byte contents (each line terminated by a single \n, no trailing blank line):

1:COMPRESS:alpha\n
2:COMPRESS:beta\n
3:COMPRESS:gamma\n
4:DELETE_DIR:beta\n
5:EXTRACT:beta.tar.gz\n
6:VERIFY_ARCHIVES:alpha.tar.gz,beta.tar.gz,gamma.tar.gz\n
7:VERIFY_DIRS:alpha,beta,gamma\n
